heading: Recreational trails program
label: 206 Recreational trails program

Prior ProvisionsA prior section 206, , , , provided for use of funds for construction and improvement of park roads and trails and for administration of such funds according to regulations jointly approved by the Secretary and the Secretary of the Interior, prior to repeal by , , .Pub. L. 85–76772 Stat. 908Pub. L. 97–424, title I, § 126(d)96 Stat. 2115Aug. 27, 1958Jan. 6, 1983

Text read as follows: “A State recreational trail advisory committee established under subsection (c)(2) may waive, in whole or in part, the requirements of clauses (ii) and (iii) of subparagraph (A) if the State recreational trail advisory committee determines and notifies the Secretary that the State does not have sufficient projects to meet the requirements of clauses (ii) and (iii) of subparagraph (A).”Pub. Nonhighway Recreational Fuel Study, , , provided that:Pub. L. 117–58, div. A, title I, § 11512135 Stat. 594Nov. 15, 2021“(a).—DefinitionsIn this section:“(1).—Highway trust fundThe term ‘Highway Trust Fund’ means the Highway Trust Fund established by section 9503(a) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [].26 U.S.C. 9503(a)“(2).—Nonhighway recreational fuel taxesThe term ‘nonhighway recreational fuel taxes’ means taxes under section[s] 4041 and 4081 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [, 4081] with respect to fuel used in vehicles on recreational trails or back country terrain (including vehicles registered for highway use when used on recreational trails, trail access roads not eligible for funding under title 23, United States Code, or back country terrain).26 U.S.C. 4041“(3).—Recreational trails programThe term ‘recreational trails program’ means the recreational trails program under , United States Code.section 206 of title 23“(b)Assessment; Report.—“(1).—AssessmentNot later than 1 year after the date of enactment of this Act [] and not less frequently than once every 5 years thereafter, as determined by the Secretary [of Transportation], the Secretary shall carry out an assessment of the best available estimate of the total amount of nonhighway recreational fuel taxes received by the Secretary of the Treasury and transferred to the Highway Trust Fund for the period covered by the assessment.Nov. 15, 2021“(2).—ReportAfter carrying out each assessment under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall submit to the Committees on Finance and Environment and Public Works of the Senate and the Committees on Ways and Means and Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives a report that includes—“(A)to assist Congress in determining an appropriate funding level for the recreational trails program—“(i)a description of the results of the assessment; and“(ii)an evaluation of whether the current recreational trails program funding level reflects the amount of nonhighway recreational fuel taxes collected and transferred to the Highway Trust Fund; and“(B)in the case of the first report submitted under this paragraph, an estimate of the frequency with which the Secretary anticipates carrying out the assessment under paragraph (1), subject to the condition that such an assessment shall be carried out not less frequently than once every 5 years.“(c).—ConsultationIn carrying out an assessment under subsection (b)(1), the Secretary may consult with, as the Secretary determines to be appropriate—“(1)the heads of—“(A)State agencies designated by Governors pursuant to , United States Code, to administer the recreational trails program; andsection 206(c)(1) of title 23“(B)division offices of the Department [of Transportation];“(2)the Secretary of the Treasury;“(3)the Administrator of the Federal Highway Administration; and“(4)groups representing recreational activities and interests, including hiking, biking and mountain biking, horseback riding, water trails, snowshoeing, cross-country skiing, snowmobiling, off-highway motorcycling, all-terrain vehicles and other offroad motorized vehicle activities, and recreational trail advocates.”

Use of Youth Service and Conservation Corps, , , provided that:Pub. L. 112–141, div. A, title I, § 1524126 Stat. 580July 6, 2012“(a).—In GeneralThe Secretary shall encourage the States and regional transportation planning agencies to enter into contracts and cooperative agreements with qualified youth service or conservation corps, as defined in sections 122(a)(2) of () and 106(c)(3) of () to perform appropriate projects eligible under sections 162, 206, [former] 213, and 217 of title 23, United States Code, and under section 1404 of the SAFETEA–LU ().Public Law 101–61042 U.S.C. 12572(a)(2)Public Law 103–8242 U.S.C. 12656(c)(3)119 Stat. 1228“(b).—RequirementsUnder any contract or cooperative agreement entered into with a qualified youth service or conservation corps under this section, the Secretary shall—“(1)set the amount of a living allowance or rate of pay for each participant in such corps at—“(A)such amount or rate as required under State law in a State with such requirements; or“(B)for corps in States not described in subparagraph (A), at such amount or rate as determined by the Secretary, not to exceed the maximum living allowance authorized by (); andsection 140 of Public Law 101–61042 U.S.C. 12594“(2)not subject such corps to the requirements of , United States Code.”section 112 of title 23Similar provisions were contained in the following prior acts:, , .Pub. L. 109–59, title I, § 1109(f)119 Stat. 1170Aug. 10, 2005, , .Pub. L. 105–178, title I, § 1112(e)112 Stat. 151June 9, 1998

children.heading: .—Motorized recreation
children.label: 1 .—Motorized recreation
children.text: The term “motorized recreation” means off-road recreation using any motor-powered vehicle, except for a motorized wheelchair.

children.label: A
children.text: pedestrian activities, including wheelchair use;

children.label: B
children.text: skating or skateboarding;

children.label: C
children.text: equestrian activities, including carriage driving;

children.label: D
children.text: nonmotorized snow trail activities, including skiing;

children.label: E
children.text: bicycling or use of other human-powered vehicles;

children.label: F
children.text: aquatic or water activities; and

children.label: G
children.text: motorized vehicular activities, including all-terrain vehicle riding, motorcycling, snowmobiling, use of off-road light trucks, or use of other off-road motorized vehicles.

children.heading: .—Program
children.label: b .—Program
children.text: In accordance with this section, the Secretary, in consultation with the Secretary of the Interior and the Secretary of Agriculture, shall carry out a program to provide and maintain recreational trails.
the Governor of the State shall designate the State agency or agencies that will be responsible for administering apportionments made to the State under this section; and

the State shall establish a State recreational trail advisory committee that represents both motorized and nonmotorized recreational trail users, which shall meet not less often than once per fiscal year.

children.label: A
children.text: have been planned and developed under the laws, policies, and administrative procedures of the State; and

children.label: B
children.text: are identified in, or further a specific goal of, a recreational trail plan, or a statewide comprehensive outdoor recreation plan required by chapter 2003 of title 54, that is in effect.

children.label: A
children.text: maintenance and restoration of existing recreational trails;

children.label: B
children.text: development and rehabilitation of trailside and trailhead facilities and trail linkages for recreational trails;

children.label: C
children.text: purchase and lease of recreational trail construction and maintenance equipment;

children.label: i
children.text: permissible under other law;

children.label: ii
children.text: necessary and recommended by a statewide comprehensive outdoor recreation plan that is required by chapter 2003 of title 54 and that is in effect;

children.label: iii
children.text: approved by the administering agency of the State designated under subsection (c)(1); and

children.label: iv
children.text: approved by each Federal agency having jurisdiction over the affected lands under such terms and conditions as the head of the Federal agency determines to be appropriate, except that the approval shall be contingent on compliance by the Federal agency with all applicable laws, including the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.), the Forest and Rangeland Renewable Resources Planning Act of 1974 ( et seq.), and the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976 ( et seq.);42 U.S.C. 432116 U.S.C. 160043 U.S.C. 1701

children.label: E
children.text: acquisition of easements and fee simple title to property for recreational trails or recreational trail corridors;

children.label: F
children.text: assessment of trail conditions for accessibility and maintenance;

children.label: G
children.text: development and dissemination of publications and operation of educational programs to promote safety and environmental protection, (as those objectives relate to one or more of the uses of recreational trails, supporting non-law enforcement trail safety and trail use monitoring patrol programs, and providing trail-related training), but in an amount not to exceed 5 percent of the apportionment made to the State for the fiscal year; and

children.label: H
children.text: payment of costs to the State incurred in administering the program, but in an amount not to exceed 7 percent of the apportionment made to the State for the fiscal year.

children.label: i
children.text: 40 percent shall be used for recreational trail or related projects that facilitate diverse recreational trail use within a recreational trail corridor, trailside, or trailhead, regardless of whether the project is for diverse motorized use, for diverse nonmotorized use, or to accommodate both motorized and nonmotorized recreational trail use;

children.label: ii
children.text: 30 percent shall be used for uses relating to motorized recreation; and

children.label: iii
children.text: 30 percent shall be used for uses relating to nonmotorized recreation.

children.heading: .—Small state exclusion
children.label: B .—Small state exclusion
children.text: Any State with a total land area of less than 3,500,000 acres shall be exempt from the requirements of clauses (ii) and (iii) of subparagraph (A).

children.heading: .—State administrative costs
children.label: C .—State administrative costs
children.text: State administrative costs eligible for funding under paragraph (2)(H) shall be exempt from the requirements of subparagraph (A).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: A State may use funds apportioned to the State to carry out this section to make grants to private organizations, municipal, county, State, and Federal Government entities, and other government entities as approved by the State after considering guidance from the State recreational trail advisory committee established under subsection (c)(2), for uses consistent with this section.

children.heading: .—Compliance
children.label: B .—Compliance
children.text: A State that makes grants under subparagraph (A) shall establish measures to verify that recipients of the grants comply with the conditions of the program for the use of grant funds.

children.heading: .—Environmental Benefit or Mitigation
children.label: e .—Environmental Benefit or Mitigation
children.text: To the extent practicable and consistent with the other requirements of this section, a State should give consideration to project proposals that provide for the redesign, reconstruction, nonroutine maintenance, or relocation of recreational trails to benefit the natural environment or to mitigate and minimize the impact to the natural environment.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Subject to the other provisions of this subsection, the Federal share of the cost of a project and the Federal share of the administrative costs of a State under this section shall be determined in accordance with section 120(b).

children.label: A
children.text: the share attributable to the Secretary of Transportation may not exceed the amount determined in accordance with section 120(b) for the cost of a project under this section; and

children.label: B
children.text: the share attributable to the Secretary and the Federal agency sponsoring the project may not exceed 95 percent of the cost of a project under this section.

children.heading: .—Use of funds from federal programs to provide non-federal share
children.label: 3 .—Use of funds from federal programs to provide non-federal share
A
children.text: expended in accordance with the requirements of the Federal program relating to activities funded and populations served; and

children.label: B
children.text: expended on a project that is eligible for assistance under this section.

children.heading: .—Use of recreational trails program funds to match other federal program funds
children.label: 4 .—Use of recreational trails program funds to match other federal program funds
A
children.text: expended in accordance with the requirements of the Federal program relating to activities funded and populations served; and

children.label: B
children.text: expended on a project that is eligible for assistance under this section.

children.heading: .—Programmatic non-federal share
children.label: 5 .—Programmatic non-federal share
children.text: A State may allow adjustments to the non-Federal share of an individual project for a fiscal year under this section if the Federal share of the cost of all projects carried out by the State under the program (excluding projects funded under paragraph (2) or (3)) using funds apportioned to the State for the fiscal year does not exceed the Federal share as determined in accordance with section 120(b).
condemnation of any kind of interest in property;

children.label: A
children.text: the land has been designated for uses other than wilderness by an approved forest land and resource management plan or has been released to uses other than wilderness by an Act of Congress; and

children.label: B
children.text: the construction is otherwise consistent with the management direction in the approved forest land and resource management plan;

children.label: A
children.text: has been designated for uses other than wilderness by an approved Bureau of Land Management resource management plan or has been released to uses other than wilderness by an Act of Congress; and

children.label: B
children.text: the construction is otherwise consistent with the management direction in the approved management plan; or

upgrading, expanding, or otherwise facilitating motorized use or access to recreational trails predominantly used by nonmotorized recreational trail users and on which, as of , motorized use was prohibited or had not occurred.May 1, 1991

children.heading: Credit for donations of funds, materials, services, or new right-of-way.—
children.label: 1 Credit for donations of funds, materials, services, or new right-of-way.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Nothing in this title or other law shall prevent a project sponsor from offering to donate funds, materials, services, or a new right-of-way for the purposes of a project eligible for assistance under this section. Any funds, or the fair market value of any materials, services, or new right-of-way, may be donated by any project sponsor and shall be credited to the non-Federal share in accordance with subsection (f).

children.heading: .—Federal project sponsors
children.label: B .—Federal project sponsors
children.text: Any funds or the fair market value of any materials or services may be provided by a Federal project sponsor and shall be credited to the Federal agency’s share in accordance with subsection (f).

children.heading: .—Planning and environmental assessment costs incurred prior to project approval
children.label: C .—Planning and environmental assessment costs incurred prior to project approval
children.text: The Secretary may allow preapproval planning and environmental compliance costs to be credited toward the non-Federal share of the cost of a project described in subsection (d)(2) (other than subparagraph (H)) in accordance with subsection (f), limited to costs incurred less than 18 months prior to project approval.

children.heading: .—Recreational purpose
children.label: 2 .—Recreational purpose
children.text: A project funded under this section is intended to enhance recreational opportunity and is not subject to or .section 138 of this titlesection 303 of title 49

children.heading: .—Continuing recreational use
children.label: 3 .—Continuing recreational use
children.text: At the option of each State, funds apportioned to the State to carry out this section may be treated as Land and Water Conservation Fund apportionments for the purposes of .section 200305(f)(3) of title 54

children.heading: .—Written assurances
children.label: A .—Written assurances
children.text: As a condition of making available apportionments for work on recreational trails that would affect privately owned land, a State shall obtain written assurances that the owner of the land will cooperate with the State and participate as necessary in the activities to be conducted.

children.heading: .—Public access
children.label: B .—Public access
children.text: Any use of the apportionments to a State to carry out this section on privately owned land must be accompanied by an easement or other legally binding agreement that ensures public access to the recreational trail improvements funded by the apportionments.

children.heading: .—Contract Authority
children.label: i .—Contract Authority
children.text: Funds authorized to carry out this section shall be available for obligation in the same manner as if the funds were apportioned under chapter 1, except that the Federal share of the cost of a project under this section shall be determined in accordance with this section.

children.heading: .—Use of Other Apportioned Funds
children.label: j .—Use of Other Apportioned Funds
children.text: Funds apportioned to a State under section 104(b) that are obligated for a recreational trail or a related project shall be administered as if the funds were made available to carry out this section.
